items.headline: October 2026 BTO Launch: Six Sites Including Bayshore, Caldecott and Chencharu Reviewed
items.summary: Stacked Homes reviews all six sites in the upcoming October 2026 BTO exercise, covering Bayshore, Caldecott, Mattar, Tengah, Chencharu and Sembawang North. The breakdown looks at accessibility, surrounding amenities and what buyers can reasonably expect from each location.
items.meaning: If you are planning to ballot in October, this is the starting point to figure out which site is actually worth your application.
items.body:
The October 2026 BTO exercise covers six sites spread across very different parts of Singapore. You are looking at Bayshore and Caldecott on the city fringe, Mattar near Bidadari, Tengah in the west, Chencharu up north, and Sembawang North near the coast. Each comes with its own set of trade-offs on transport, surrounding amenities and how built-up the neighbourhood already is.

Bayshore sits close to East Coast Park and the upcoming Bayshore MRT station on the Thomson-East Coast Line, which makes it one of the more sought-after sites but prices will reflect that. Caldecott offers a quieter, greener setting with existing MRT access. Mattar benefits from a mature estate around it. Tengah and Chencharu are still being developed from scratch, while Sembawang North suits buyers who are comfortable being further from the city.

With six sites on the table, the key is to be honest about your priorities before you ballot. Location and transport access matter most for long-term resale value, while existing amenities affect your day-to-day from the moment you collect your keys. Running through each site against your own checklist will serve you better than simply going for the lowest price tier.
items.headline_zh: 2026年10月BTO推出：Bayshore、Caldecott和Chencharu等六个地点全解析
items.summary_zh: Stacked Homes详细解析即将到来的2026年10月BTO申购活动中所有六个地点，包括Bayshore、Caldecott、Mattar、Tengah、Chencharu和Sembawang North。分析涵盖交通便利性、周边配套设施，以及买家对各地点可以合理期待的条件。
items.meaning_zh: 如果你打算在10月份参加抽签，这篇文章是你判断哪个地点真正值得申请的最佳起点。
items.body_zh:
2026年10月的BTO申购活动共涵盖六个地点，分布在新加坡各不相同的区域。包括靠近市区边缘的Bayshore和Caldecott、位于Bidadari附近的Mattar、西部的Tengah、北部的Chencharu，以及靠近海岸的Sembawang North。每个地点在交通、周边配套和周围成熟程度方面各有利弊。

Bayshore靠近东海岸公园，以及即将开通的汤申-东海岸线（Thomson-East Coast Line）Bayshore MRT站，因此备受追捧，价格自然也会反映这一点。Caldecott环境较为清静，绿意盎然，现有MRT接驳也方便。Mattar周围是成熟组屋区，配套齐全。Tengah和Chencharu目前仍在从零开发阶段，而Sembawang North则适合不介意离市区较远的买家。

面对六个地点，最重要的是在抽签前先想清楚自己真正在乎什么。地点和交通配套对长期转售价值影响最大，而现有配套设施则从你拿到钥匙那天起就直接影响日常生活。逐一对照自己的需求清单，比单纯追求最低价格层级来得踏实。

items.headline: Too Old for a Bank Loan and Sitting on a $1.7M Condo: Should You Downsize to a Resale HDB?
items.summary: A homeowner who fully owns a $1.7M condo but can no longer qualify for a bank loan is weighing whether to sell and move into a resale HDB flat to unlock cash. The piece works through the eligibility rules, tax implications and lifestyle trade-offs involved.
items.meaning: If you are near retirement with a paid-up private home, this maps out the real rules and maths around selling down to an HDB flat.
items.body:
Owning a $1.7 million condo outright sounds like a strong position, but once you no longer qualify for a bank loan, your options narrow quickly. Selling the private property and buying a resale HDB flat is one of the cleaner paths to unlocking that equity while still having a home to live in through retirement.

The rules have real teeth here. You can buy a resale HDB flat after selling your private property, but you must use it as your primary residence and cannot hold another private property at the same time. Depending on your citizenship status and whether you have previously owned an HDB flat, the 15-month wait-out period may also apply. On top of that, Buyer's Stamp Duty applies to the resale flat purchase, and CPF withdrawal rules will affect how much of your sale proceeds you can keep liquid.

The numbers can still make strong sense for the right person. Selling at $1.7 million and buying a resale flat for, say, $700,000 to $800,000 frees up a significant cash buffer for retirement. The honest trade-off is lifestyle: you give up condo facilities and the status that comes with private property. But for many near-retirement homeowners, getting that capital working for them matters more than keeping a pool they rarely use.

If this scenario fits your situation, speak to both a financial adviser and a property lawyer before you act. The order in which you sell and buy, and what you do with CPF proceeds, has implications that vary depending on your exact circumstances and age.
items.headline_zh: 年纪太大申请不到银行贷款，手持一套$1.7M的condo：该换购转售HDB吗？
items.summary_zh: 一名屋主全款持有一套$1.7M的condo，但已无法申请银行贷款，正在考虑是否卖掉私宅、换买转售HDB组屋来套现。文章详细梳理了相关资格条件、税务影响以及生活方式上的取舍。
items.meaning_zh: 如果你即将退休且私宅已还清，这篇文章为你清楚列出卖私宅换HDB组屋的实际规则和算法。
items.body_zh:
全款持有一套$1.7 million的condo听起来底气十足，但一旦你无法再申请银行贷款，选择就会大幅收窄。卖掉私宅、购入转售HDB组屋，是其中较为清晰的一条路，既能套现资产，又能在退休后继续有地方住。

相关规定有一定约束力。你可以在卖掉私宅后购买转售HDB组屋，但必须以此为主要住所，且不能同时持有其他私人住宅。根据你的公民身份及是否曾拥有HDB组屋，15个月的等待期也可能适用。此外，购买转售组屋须缴交BSD，而CPF提款规定也会影响你能保留多少流动资金。

对合适的人来说，这笔账依然划得来。以$1.7 million卖出，再以$700,000到$800,000左右买入转售组屋，能腾出相当可观的退休现金储备。真正的取舍在于生活方式：你要放弃condo设施以及拥有私宅所带来的身份感。但对许多即将退休的屋主而言，让这笔资本发挥效用，远比保留一个很少使用的游泳池来得重要。

如果这个情况符合你的处境，在行动之前，建议同时咨询理财顾问和产业律师。卖出和买入的先后顺序，以及如何处理CPF收益，都会因个人具体情况和年龄不同而有所差异。

items.headline: HDB Resale Prices Have Fallen for Two Consecutive Quarters: What It Means If You Want to Sell
items.summary: HDB resale flat prices have now declined for two quarters in a row, a clear shift from the strong gains seen in recent years. The article weighs whether this is a brief correction or the start of a sustained cooling.
items.meaning: Sellers should know the market has turned softer and buyers have more room to negotiate than they did a year ago.
items.body:
HDB resale prices have now fallen for two consecutive quarters, a clear break from the sustained gains the market delivered in recent years. This is not a freefall, but the direction has shifted and the days of sellers reliably getting more than their asking price are fading.

The cooling measures introduced over the past few years are doing their intended job. Higher Additional Buyer's Stamp Duty rates, tighter loan-to-value limits, and a larger pipeline of BTO flats coming through have all taken pressure off the resale market. Whether this turns out to be a short correction before prices stabilise, or the start of a longer soft patch, depends partly on how much new supply keeps coming and how economic conditions hold up.

For sellers, the practical message is to price realistically from the start. Overpriced units are sitting on the market longer, and buyers now have genuine room to negotiate in a way they did not a year or two ago. Setting expectations correctly matters more than ever.

For buyers, two soft quarters is not a signal to wait indefinitely. The fundamentals that make a flat worth buying, meaning location, flat type and remaining lease, have not changed. If a flat suits your needs and is priced fairly, the current market gives you leverage to negotiate rather than a reason to keep sitting on the sidelines.
items.headline_zh: HDB转售价格连续两个季度下跌：有意出售的屋主该怎么看
items.summary_zh: HDB转售组屋价格已连续两个季度下滑，与近年来的强劲涨势明显背道而驰。文章探讨这究竟是短暂调整，还是持续降温的开端。
items.meaning_zh: 卖家要清楚，市场已趋于疲软，买家目前的议价空间比一年前大得多。
items.body_zh:
HDB转售价格已连续两个季度下跌，与近年来市场持续上涨的走势明显不同。这并非急剧下跌，但方向已经转变，卖家轻松超出要价成交的时代正在消退。

过去几年推出的降温措施正在发挥预期效果。更高的ABSD税率、更严格的贷款比例上限，以及持续增加的BTO供应，都在为转售市场减压。至于这是价格在企稳前的短暂调整，还是较长期疲软的开始，部分取决于新供应的持续情况以及整体经济走势。

对卖家而言，实际的建议是从一开始就定出合理价格。定价过高的单位在市场上挂牌时间越来越长，买家现在确实拥有一两年前所没有的议价空间。正确设定预期，比以往任何时候都更重要。

对买家而言，两个季度的疲软并不意味着可以无限期观望。决定一套组屋是否值得购买的基本因素，地点、房型和剩余租约年限，并没有改变。如果一套组屋符合你的需求且定价合理，当前市场给你的是谈判筹码，而不是继续旁观的理由。

items.headline: Bayshore Drive Mixed-Use Site Awarded to Frasers Consortium at $1,323 Per Square Foot
items.summary: A government land sale site at Bayshore Drive has gone to a Frasers-led group at $1,323 per square foot per plot ratio, setting the cost benchmark for what will eventually become a mixed residential and commercial precinct near the upcoming Bayshore MRT station.
items.meaning: This land price signals that new condos at Bayshore will likely launch at a significant premium, so factor that in if you are eyeing this area.
items.body:
A Frasers-led consortium has won the government land sale site at Bayshore Drive with a bid of $1,323 per square foot per plot ratio. This figure sets the cost benchmark for the land alone, before a single brick is laid, any unit is marketed, or a developer margin is added. The site will eventually become a mixed residential and commercial development near the upcoming Bayshore MRT station on the Thomson-East Coast Line.

Bayshore is a new precinct still taking shape. Its appeal is the combination of proximity to East Coast Park, a fresh MRT connection and a neighbourhood that has not yet been fully built out. The mixed-use element means the final development will likely include retail and amenities at ground level alongside residential units, which tends to make a precinct more liveable over time.

For buyers tracking this area, the land price is a useful signal. With the developer paying $1,323 psf just for the plot, launch prices for residential units will almost certainly start well above $2,000 psf and could run considerably higher depending on unit mix and market conditions at the time of launch. Anyone considering Bayshore should factor in that premium now rather than being surprised when the project comes to market.
items.headline_zh: Bayshore Drive综合用途地段由Frasers财团以每平方英尺$1,323夺标
items.summary_zh: Bayshore Drive的政府售地地段由Frasers领导的财团以每容积率平方英尺$1,323夺得，为这个未来将成为Bayshore MRT站附近住宅与商业综合发展区的项目设定了土地成本基准。
items.meaning_zh: 这个地价意味着Bayshore的新condo上市时很可能带有相当高的溢价，如果你正在关注这一带，要把这个因素纳入考量。
items.body_zh:
由Frasers领导的财团以每容积率平方英尺$1,323的出价，赢得了Bayshore Drive的政府售地地段。这个数字是纯粹的土地成本基准，在任何一块砖头砌上去、任何单位开始销售或加入发展商利润之前。这块地段最终将发展成为汤申-东海岸线（Thomson-East Coast Line）即将开通的Bayshore MRT站附近的住宅与商业综合发展项目。

Bayshore是一个仍在成形中的新区域，吸引力在于毗邻东海岸公园、即将拥有全新MRT连接，以及整个区域尚未完全发展成熟。综合用途的定位意味着最终建成的项目很可能在住宅单位之外，底层也会有零售商店和配套设施，这往往会让一个区域随着时间推移变得更宜居。

对于关注这一带的买家来说，地价是一个有用的参考信号。发展商单是土地就已支付$1,323 psf，住宅单位的开盘价几乎可以确定会远超$2,000 psf，而且根据单位组合和上市时的市场状况，价格可能还会高出不少。任何考虑Bayshore的人，现在就应该把这个溢价纳入考量，而不是等到项目推出时才感到意外。

items.headline: Tan Boon Liat Building Sells for $950M: What Might Replace This Rare Freehold Site
items.summary: The freehold Tan Boon Liat Building along Outram Road has changed hands for $950 million in one of Singapore's largest recent collective sales. Given its freehold tenure and central location, the site is expected to be redeveloped into a high-value mixed-use project.
items.meaning: A $950 million land price near the CBD confirms that developers still see strong demand for prime freehold Singapore property, which tends to push up values in the surrounding area.
items.body:
Tan Boon Liat Building along Outram Road has changed hands for $950 million in one of the largest collective sale transactions Singapore has seen in recent years. The building is freehold, which is genuinely rare for a site of this size and central location, and sits close to the CBD and the emerging Greater Southern Waterfront corridor.

For the owners who agreed to sell, the collective payout will be substantial. For the developer who acquires it, the calculation is straightforward: redevelop the site into something that justifies paying $950 million for the land alone. Given freehold tenure and a location of this quality, a high-value mixed-use project targeting both residential buyers and commercial tenants is the expected outcome.

For everyone else who owns property in the surrounding area, a deal at this scale reinforces the value story nearby. Developers do not spend close to a billion dollars on a site unless they have strong conviction about demand for what gets built there. It does not guarantee that prices in Outram or Tanjong Pagar will move overnight, but it keeps the underlying confidence in Singapore's prime city-fringe market firmly intact.
items.headline_zh: 丹文利亚大厦以$950M易手：这块罕见永久地契地段将变身何物
items.summary_zh: 位于Outram Road的永久地契丹文利亚大厦（Tan Boon Liat Building）以$950 million易手，是新加坡近年来规模最大的en bloc成交之一。鉴于其永久地契性质和中央位置，该地段预计将重新发展为高价值综合用途项目。
items.meaning_zh: CBD附近$950 million的地价证实，发展商对新加坡优质永久地契房产的需求依然强劲，这往往会带动周边区域的房产价值上升。
items.body_zh:
位于Outram Road的丹文利亚大厦（Tan Boon Liat Building）以$950 million易手，是新加坡近年来规模最大的en bloc交易之一。该大厦属于永久地契，对于这种规模和中央位置的地段而言实属罕见，且毗邻CBD以及正在发展中的大南部滨水区（Greater Southern Waterfront）一带。

对于同意出售的业主来说，集体分成相当可观。对于收购方发展商而言，计算方式很直接：将地段重新发展成能够支撑单是土地就花了$950 million的项目。鉴于永久地契性质和这一位置的优越条件，预计最终将打造出针对住宅买家和商业租户的高价值综合用途项目。

对于在周边持有房产的其他人而言，这样规模的交易进一步强化了附近的价值故事。发展商不会花近十亿元买下一块地，除非他们对未来建成项目的需求有充分信心。这并不保证Outram或丹戎巴葛（Tanjong Pagar）的房价会一夜之间上涨，但确实让新加坡优质市区边缘市场的整体信心保持稳固。

items.headline: Analysis: Singapore Condos With the Worst Rental Yields Share One Key Trait
items.summary: A data-driven look at condo rental yields across Singapore found that the lowest-performing projects consistently share a common characteristic, whether that is oversupply, weak location fundamentals or inflated purchase prices. The findings give buyers a framework for spotting which condos to avoid.
items.meaning: If rental return matters when you buy a condo, knowing what drags yields down can stop you from overpaying for a unit that barely covers its own costs.
items.body:
Data on condo rental yields across Singapore shows a consistent pattern among the worst performers. The projects that deliver the thinnest returns tend to share at least one of three problems: too many similar units competing for the same pool of tenants in the same corridor, a purchase price that was too high relative to achievable rents from the start, or a location that simply does not draw strong rental demand.

Oversupply is a quiet killer. A corridor that sees four or five large condo launches within a short period will have landlords competing against each other, which pushes rents down even as purchase prices stay elevated. Pair that with a project that is not within easy walking distance of an MRT station or a major employment centre, and you have the formula for yields that barely cover costs.

The practical takeaway for anyone buying a condo with rental income in mind is to run the numbers before you commit, not after. Take a realistic monthly rent figure for that unit type and location, multiply by 12, and divide by your total purchase cost including stamp duties. If the gross yield comes in below 2.5 percent, the numbers are already thin and any vacancy period or maintenance cost will eat whatever is left. Knowing what drags yield down gives you a checklist to work through before you sign.
items.headline_zh: 分析：新加坡租金回报最差的condo有一个共同特征
items.summary_zh: 一项以数据为依据的新加坡condo租金回报率分析发现，表现最差的项目始终有一个共同特征，无论是供应过剩、地点基本面薄弱，还是购买价格虚高。这些发现为买家提供了一个识别哪些condo应该避开的参考框架。
items.meaning_zh: 如果你买condo时看重租金回报，了解什么因素会拖低回报率，能让你避免以过高价格买入一个几乎无法覆盖自身成本的单位。
items.body_zh:
新加坡各地condo租金回报率数据显示，表现最差的项目有一个规律性模式。回报最薄的项目，往往至少存在以下三个问题之一：同一地带有太多相似单位争夺同一批租客，导致供过于求；从一开始购买价格相对于可实现租金就已过高；或者所在位置根本无法吸引强劲的租赁需求。

供应过剩是个无声杀手。一条走廊在短期内相继推出四五个大型condo项目，业主之间相互竞争就会压低租金，而购买价格却依然居高不下。如果再加上项目距离MRT站或主要就业中心步行不便，那就是租金回报率勉强覆盖成本的典型组合。

对任何有意以租金收入为目的购买condo的人来说，实际的建议是在决定之前就算清楚数字，而不是之后。以该单位类型和地点的实际月租金为基准，乘以12，再除以包含印花税在内的总购买成本。如果毛回报率低于2.5%，数字已经很薄，任何空租期或维修费用都会蚕食所剩无几的收益。了解什么因素会拖低回报率，能给你提供一份在签字前逐一核对的清单。

items.headline: Lentor Gardens Residences Moves 270 Units at Average $2,350 Per Square Foot
items.summary: Lentor Gardens Residences sold 270 units on its launch weekend at an average of $2,350 per square foot, showing continued buyer appetite in the Lentor Hills corridor despite softer sentiment elsewhere. The take-up rate suggests this part of the north has held its appeal.
items.meaning: If you are looking at Lentor, do not count on prices easing much, as buyers are still paying above $2,300 per square foot here.
items.body:
Lentor Gardens Residences sold 270 units over its launch weekend at an average price of $2,350 per square foot. That level of take-up in a single weekend shows that buyer appetite in the Lentor Hills corridor has not faded, even as sentiment in other parts of the market has softened.

The Lentor area has now seen several launches, each priced progressively higher than the last, and buyers have kept showing up. The Lentor MRT station, the greenery-focused planning of the area and the appeal of a freshly built township environment have made this stretch attractive to families and upgraders willing to pay for a newer product in a well-planned setting.

If you were holding out for Lentor prices to ease as more supply comes in, the sales figures suggest patience alone is unlikely to get you a cheaper entry point. At $2,350 psf average, this is premium pricing for a location outside the central region. Anyone seriously considering Lentor should look honestly at whether the lifestyle and locational factors justify the cost, rather than expecting the market to move down to meet them.
items.headline_zh: Lentor Gardens Residences开盘周末售出270个单位，平均每平方英尺$2,350
items.summary_zh: Lentor Gardens Residences在开盘周末售出270个单位，平均价格为每平方英尺$2,350，显示尽管其他地区市场情绪趋于平淡，Lentor Hills一带的买家兴趣依然持续。认购率说明北部这片区域依然保有吸引力。
items.meaning_zh: 如果你在考虑Lentor，不要指望价格会有太大松动，因为买家在这里依然愿意支付超过每平方英尺$2,300的价格。
items.body_zh:
Lentor Gardens Residences在开盘周末售出270个单位，平均价格为每平方英尺$2,350。单一周末就能达到这样的认购量，说明即使市场其他地区情绪趋于疲软，Lentor Hills一带的买家兴趣依然未减。

Lentor一带已陆续推出多个项目，每次推出的价格都比上一次更高，但买家依然接连入场。Lentor MRT站、区域以绿化为主的规划理念，以及全新市镇环境的吸引力，让这片区域对愿意为优质新产品付出溢价的家庭和提升型买家颇具吸引力。

如果你一直在等待Lentor价格随着更多供应涌入而回落，这份销售数据说明，单靠等待不大可能换来更便宜的入场机会。以平均$2,350 psf来看，这对于中央区以外的地点而言是相当高的定价。任何认真考虑Lentor的人，都应该坦诚地评估生活方式和地点因素是否值得这个价格，而不是指望市场向下调整来迁就自己。

items.headline: Most Buyers of Million-Dollar HDB Flats in 2025 Paid No Cash Over Valuation
items.summary: PropNex data shows that the majority of buyers who purchased HDB resale flats above $1 million in 2025 did not pay any cash over what the bank valued the flat at. Bank valuations have largely caught up with the high prices being transacted in this segment.
items.meaning: If you assumed million-dollar HDB deals always require extra cash on top of what the bank lends, the data says that is no longer the norm.
items.body:
When an HDB flat sells above $1 million, many people assume the buyer must be handing over extra cash on top of what the bank values the flat at. That gap is called cash over valuation, or COV. But PropNex data covering 2025 transactions shows that the majority of buyers in the million-dollar HDB resale segment paid no COV at all.

The reason is that bank valuations have caught up with what the market is actually transacting at in this segment. As million-dollar HDB resale deals have become more frequent, valuers have adjusted their benchmarks to reflect the new normal. Buyers are largely financing these purchases through their loan and CPF in the conventional way, without needing to come up with an extra pile of cash outside the formal transaction.

This matters for anyone who has been sitting out the market on the assumption that buying at the top end of the HDB resale range would require a large cash top-up they did not have. The financing picture here is more straightforward than many people expect. It is still worth checking the actual valuation for the specific flat you want before assuming anything, but the data says the old rule of thumb about COV being unavoidable in this price bracket no longer holds.
items.headline_zh: 2025年大多数百万组屋买家无需支付现金超额估值
items.summary_zh: PropNex的数据显示，2025年购入价格超过$1 million的HDB转售组屋的买家，大多数都没有支付任何超出银行估值的现金。银行估值已基本追上这一价格区间的实际成交价。
items.meaning_zh: 如果你一直以为百万组屋交易总需要在银行贷款之外额外拿出现金，数据告诉你这已不再是常态。
items.body_zh:
每当一套HDB组屋以超过$1 million的价格成交，很多人都会以为买家必须在银行估值之外额外拿出现金。这个差额就叫做现金超额估值，也就是COV。但PropNex涵盖2025年交易的数据显示，百万组屋转售这一价格区间的大多数买家，根本没有支付任何COV。

原因在于，银行估值已经追上了这一价格区间的实际市场成交价。随着百万组屋转售交易变得越来越普遍，估价师也相应调整了基准，以反映新的市场常态。买家大多以常规方式通过贷款和CPF完成这些购买，不需要在正式交易之外额外筹备一大笔现金。

这对那些一直因为担心需要拿出大笔现金补差价而在场外观望的人来说，是个重要信息。实际上，这里的融资情况比很多人预想的要简单得多。在买任何特定组屋之前，仍然值得核实一下实际估值，但数据说明，那条关于这一价格区间COV无可避免的老经验，已经不再适用了。
